title: Root
description: How do we make pixelpack encode real programs efficiently? Current state: 3-254 byte programs encoded as multi-pixel PNGs. 254-byte fibonacci needs 56 pixels (1792 bits) which is terrible compression. Each pixel uses 1 of 16 strategies to expand a 4-bit seed + 28 data bits into a short byte string. Next leap: fractal unfold where pixels reference each other, better cross-strategy composition, or fundamentally different encoding approaches. What paths lead to the biggest capability gains?
